items.rationale: House's strongest conviction: declared the 'killer app' that has reached product-market fit as L2 fees fell below a penny; seen as the on-ramp to bring billions onchain and a national-security lever for USD dominance.
items.age_note: Building since 2023 ('largest use cases of crypto'), but PMF declared firmly in 2024 — conviction strengthened over time

citations.quote: Stablecoins are the "killer app" of crypto, and here's a bunch of great data that shows it.
Stablecoin metrics are at all time highs... Bringing down transaction fees has made stablecoins a killer app.
Stablecoin payments could be the answer to how we bring billions of people onchain.

items.rationale: Viewed as the endgame for blockchain scalability and a new developer primitive; Jolt shipped as 'fastest zkVM,' prover overhead falling fast, expanding the universe of applications. Considered 'under-hyped.'

citations.quote: Today we're releasing Jolt Alpha ⚡️ ✅ Fastest zkVM in the world. ✅ Best developer experience.
I still think ZK is under hyped.
as you bring that Pro overhead down the number of applicable applications for snarks grows dramatically

items.rationale: Framed as the future of consumer crypto and the path to crypto becoming 'synonymous with the Internet'; energy specifically 'needs' decentralization. Led $9M Daylight Series A.

citations.quote: The future of consumer crypto? DePIN... Crypto will win when it's synonymous with the Internet itself
Energy is one of the few non-crypto industries that doesn't just want decentralization, but needs it
I'm excited to announce that @a16zcrypto is leading the $9M Series A round in @daylightenergy!

items.rationale: Crypto positioned as counterbalance to centralized AI: proving content authenticity against deepfakes, decentralizing models, and giving autonomous AI agents wallets — called among the clearest use cases since BTC store-of-value.
items.age_note: Thesis present since Dec 2023; sharpened in late 2024 toward AI agents + payments as concrete near-term use case

citations.quote: Stablecoin payments and AI agents are some of the clearest use cases for crypto since Bitcoin's store of value.
AI agents can't have bank accounts, but they can have crypto wallets.
Crypto can carry the torch of the open internet and help keep it creative, open, and diverse.

items.rationale: Seen as the most exciting infrastructure innovation in 15 years by the IP-focused partner; provides the coordination/attribution layer for an explosion of AI-generated content. Led $80M Series B.

citations.quote: I don't think I've ever seen an infrastructure innovation that excites me as much as @StoryProtocol.
We want to help Story become the infrastructure for AI systems and creative people to happily co-exist.

items.rationale: Endorses rollup-centric roadmap; actively investing to solve fragmentation (Espresso) and achieve seamless cross-rollup interop ('holy grail'). Urges Ethereum not to slow down on throughput.

citations.quote: Seamless interoperability is my holy grail for rollups, and T1 is working to finally make it possible.
Ethereum cannot afford to slow down.
Today, we're announcing that we're leading @EspressoSys' Series B

items.rationale: Believed to unlock '100x faster innovation' by unbundling cryptoeconomic security from the EVM — a 'verifiable cloud' extending Ethereum. Led Series B.

citations.quote: EigenLayer has the potential to unlock 100x faster innovation on technologies like consensus mechanisms, novel virtual machines, decentralized oracles, bridges
> EigenLayer is the verifiable cloud. Massive leap forward today toward the realization of that vision.

items.rationale: Held as a prerequisite for mainstream adoption; web proofs (zkTLS) seen as critical to onboarding a billion users by importing exogenous incentives; collaborative SNARKs enable private shared state.
items.age_note: Privacy-as-prerequisite stance persisted from 2023; specific tooling (zkTLS, coSNARKs) became the 2024 focus

citations.quote: I'm now convinced that web proofs/zkTLS will play a critical role in getting us there [a billion people].
Aleo's breakthrough is to use cutting edge cryptography in the form of zero knowledge proofs to create a blockchain that is privacy-preserving while still being fully verifiable.

items.rationale: Governance seen as underappreciated source of value/funding; after a 2024 court ruling exposed members to liability, legal wrappers (DUNA) became a 'strategic imperative.' Treasuries exceed annual VC inflows.

citations.quote: It's now a strategic imperative for DAO's to use legal wrappers.
DAO treasuries hold more in liquid tokens than the entire amount of VC investments into crypto companies last year.

items.rationale: Open protocols (Farcaster) seen as restoring permissionless innovation and user data ownership; vibrant dev ecosystems emerging. Core to the 'bigger pie via composability' thesis.
items.age_note: Long-held thesis (Sound.xyz, open-protocol social since 2023); restated through 2024 as ecosystems materialize

citations.quote: The Decentralized Social experiment is underway, and vibrant developer ecosystems are emerging around open protocols like Farcaster.

items.rationale: Endorsed as powerful real-time information-aggregation tools; partner repeatedly argued US restrictions degrade truth-finding and that letting Americans participate improves accuracy.
items.age_note: Reversal of older internal skepticism — a 2023 transcript noted prediction markets 'didn't find a good use case'; by 2024 the house view is clearly positive

citations.quote: Prediction markets have proven to be a powerful tool for surfacing real time information.
the odds notably shifted right after U.S. people... were allowed to legally participate.

items.rationale: With sub-penny fees, ubiquitous digital collecting / event memorabilia and creator-direct monetization seen as newly viable; royalties remain the unsolved design problem.
items.age_note: Pivoted from 2023 'IP/profile-picture' framing to 2024 mass-consumer collectible + tap-to-claim physical experiences

citations.quote: once transaction fees got small enough, blockchains would enable all manner of low-cost digital collecting experiences.
NFTs enable digital property rights in art, music, tickets, etc... This is revolutionary technology.

items.rationale: The house's loudest concern: memecoins deter great founders, give ammunition to politicized regulators, distort how the public/regulators see crypto, and set the whole industry back while adding no technical value.

citations.quote: we shouldn't pretend that the casino doesn't set us back. Deterring great founders and giving ammo to politicized regulators has a profound effect.
Memecoins are the crypto equivalent of chain emails.
When memecoins have a moment, people who know better make tortured rationalizations.

items.rationale: Through most of the corpus, the dominant anxiety: SEC weaponizing Howey, attacking exchanges/DEXs/NFT platforms, plus IRS broker/DeFi rules and debanking — all seen as politicized lawfare without congressional mandate.
items.age_note: Major reversal: hostile/defensive posture dominated through Aug 2024 ('brazen example of the SEC's use of lawfare'); after the Nov 2024 US election the house flipped to optimism ('future of crypto in the U.S. is bright'), while still warning of last-gasp enforcement

citations.quote: This is a brazen example of the SEC's use of lawfare to chill innovation.
The future of crypto in the U.S. is bright!
The broker reporting rule is an obvious example — A fantastical expansion of the words "effectuate transactions" to enable the IRS to ban DeFi.

items.rationale: Repeated, vehement warnings that short lockups and fixed token allocations enable insiders to dump on retail and misalign incentives; advocates 4yr/1yr-cliff standard and dilutable, equity-proportional allocations.

citations.quote: If a VC pushes to shorten your token lockup... what they're really telling you is "we intend to dump our bags as soon as you'll let us".
We're seeing more and more crypto startups stuck with predatory terms from early-stage deals.
Fixed token allocations are predatory.

items.rationale: Easy early access to secondary markets (ICOs, SPACs as analogs) seen as an existential drag on innovation; disclosure alone deemed insufficient. Favors long holding periods to force long-term building.

citations.quote: Unfettered access to secondary market liquidity is an existential threat to innovation.
accelerating the pathway to secondary liquidity for any asset increases the prevalence of get-rich-quick-schemes.

items.rationale: Worry that 'centralization is like gravity' — without legal incentives and a control-based decentralization standard, networks default to web2-style value extraction (Facebook/Apple/Amazon chains).

citations.quote: Decentralization is at the heart of what makes crypto worthwhile. But if we don't properly incentivize and enshrine it in law, we'll lose it.
Detractors say "decentralization" is too amorphous... They are all wrong. Decentralization still matters.

items.rationale: Despite excitement on ZK performance, the cryptography lead flags security as the single biggest threat to SNARKs realizing their potential — circuits 'riddled with bugs,' protected only by obscurity today.

citations.quote: the security issues cannot be overstated I think they are the biggest threat to snarks not fulfilling their potential
they're riddled with bugs... really if you use one today you're really protected more by obscurity

items.rationale: Recognized structural risk: PBS decentralizes validators but centralizes builders via a 'proof-of-MEV' feedback loop; no decentralized sequencer exists yet; MEV remains a tax on users.

citations.quote: PBS is reducing centralization in validation, but might create centralization in building because of this... proof of MEV competition
We don't have any decentralized sequencer, and shared sequencing networks could be first

items.rationale: Academic work in the corpus shows flight-to-safety dynamics, liquidity withdrawal exactly when needed, and cross-jurisdictional regulatory arbitrage that perpetuates run risk on 'less safe' stablecoins.
items.age_note: Stated in a Sept 2023 research transcript; not restated in 2024, when stablecoin commentary turned overwhelmingly bullish on PMF — tension persists but is no longer foregrounded

citations.quote: liquidity providers tend to remove liquidity very quickly particularly from... stable coins that are deemed to be less safe

items.rationale: Core long-term holding: ETF approvals 'enshrine' that decentralization moves it outside securities law; expected to be a deflationary currency post-merge; the base of the rollup-centric scaling thesis.

citations.quote: These approvals enshrine the SEC's position in 2018 - ETH is not a security.
long term I think most of us expect eth to be a deflationary currency

items.rationale: Respected as the foundational store-of-value and 'first Internet native religion'; valued precisely for its simplicity and ossification, though flagged as having a long-run security-budget problem.
items.age_note: Long-run concern persists: 2023-2024 statements warn its security model 'needs to change' as block rewards decline — admiration coexists with a structural caveat

citations.quote: There's value to be discovered in many areas of tradeoff space... It's incredible that we have bitcoin.
Bitcoin is arguably the most successful religion of the 21st century, and the first Internet native religion.

items.rationale: Cited repeatedly as the leading chain on adoption metrics — top chain by monthly active addresses, and (with Base) capturing most low-fee DEX volume; Phantom among fastest-growing wallets. Praise is metric-driven, not ideological.
items.age_note: Mixed signal: celebrated on adoption metrics in 2024 but the same metrics flagged as gameable, and Solana named as a venue for validator sandwiching/MEV abuse

citations.quote: The top chain by monthly active addresses: @solana.
low-transaction fee blockchains like @base and @solana captured a majority of this activity.

items.rationale: Backed as the IP coordination layer for the AI-content era; the single most exciting infrastructure bet for the IP-focused partner. $80M Series B led.

citations.quote: With the rise of AI... We desperately need an open, global coordination layer for IP, and I couldn't be more excited about what Story is building!

items.rationale: Marketplace for raw cryptoeconomic security viewed as a 100x unlock for open innovation on Ethereum; also the security backbone for newer bets (real-time proofs, T1).

citations.quote: Re-staking was the ultimate nerdsnipe for me, and I am very optimistic for the future presented by EigenDA and prolific AVS deployment.

items.rationale: Held to have no enterprise value and to exist purely for speculation; 'when that's all there is, it's a memecoin.' Seen as actively damaging industry perception, regulation, and builder behavior.

citations.quote: It's a memecoin when that's all there is.
memecoins/store of value tokens like Dogecoin have no enterprise value (like gold).

items.rationale: Research in the corpus repeatedly flags Tether reserve opacity: during stress, markets fled TO it on regulatory grounds but doubted its reserve quality and peg sustainability. Less transparent than USDC/DAI.
items.age_note: From a Sept 2023 research transcript; not restated in 2024 — stablecoins as a category turned bullish, but the transparency caveat was never retracted

citations.quote: there were a lot of concerns about the quality of the assets of tether and which also was particularly not transparent about the quality of these assets

items.rationale: Treated as a settled cautionary tale: undercollateralized algo designs are prone to death spirals; regulatory consensus (US + EU) has turned against them in favor of fiat-backed designs.
items.age_note: Lesson cemented in 2023 (Terra collapse analysis); referenced in 2024 only as historical precedent, not active debate

citations.quote: as we've seen in the context of Terra... these things could lead um very quickly to death Spirals and unravel
The SEC's victory over Terraform Labs today shouldn't surprise anyone.

risk_regime.rationale: As of 2024-12-31 the house is structurally bullish: adoption and usage metrics at all-time highs, stablecoins at PMF, infrastructure (L2 fees <1¢, zkVMs, restaking) maturing, and — decisively — a favorable regulatory turn after the Nov 2024 US election flipped years of defensive posture to 'the future of crypto in the U.S. is bright.' Risk-on is tempered, not euphoric: explicit warnings against election hype, expected last-gasp enforcement actions, ongoing debanking, and a persistent insistence on building real (decentralized, non-casino) value rather than chasing speculation.

notes: Corpus spans ~18 months (mid-2023 to 2024-12-31) across distinct voices: a regulatory/legal lead (memecoins, lockups, Howey/decentralization, lawfare), a CTO-style infra voice (ZK, DePIN, rollups, AI×crypto), a cryptography researcher (Jolt/SNARK security), an IP/marketplace economist (Story, NFTs, five-forces composability), a data/metrics voice (active users, adoption), and academic guest transcripts (stablecoin run-risk, MEV/PBS, consensus). Treated as one 'house' per instructions, but consensus is strongest on stablecoins, ZK, decentralization-as-core-value, and anti-memecoin stance; weaker/individual on some token calls. Recency weighting drove two notable reversals: (1) US regulatory posture flipped from hostile-defensive to optimistic post-election (Nov 2024); (2) prediction markets moved from internal skepticism (2023) to endorsement (2024). Stablecoin run-risk and Tether/algo-stablecoin concerns are 'persisted' from 2023 research and sit in tension with 2024's bullish stablecoin-PMF narrative — both reported. Conviction scores are intensity of expressed enthusiasm/concern, not probabilities. Note structural irony in the house view: simultaneously most excited about stablecoins/payments and most concerned about speculation/casino dynamics — the consensus is 'risk-on on utility, risk-off on speculation.'
